data.title: Adobe’s Loss, Marvell’s Win: Top Analyst Bangs the Drum for AI Chip Stock
data.description:
Marvell’s (NASDAQ:MRVL) new CFO has yet to make an impact at his new workplace, but he’s having quite an impact at his former one today.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

On Thursday, the chipmaker announced that CFO Willem Meintjes will leave his role following 10 years at the company. His position will be taken by Dan Durn, who will start his new job on June 15. Durn most recently served as CFO at Adobe between October 2021 and June 2026. Consequently, ADBE shares were taking a severe beating in Friday’s session with Durn’s exit further spooking investors already concerned about how Adobe will hold up in the new AI-driven paradigm.

Which doesn’t seem to be a concern for Marvell, a company that has emerged as one of the AI trade’s big winners, with shares up by 303% over the past year.

The good news, according to B. Riley’s Craig Ellis, an analyst who ranks among the top 1% on Wall Street, is that more gains are on the way.

Ellis reiterated a Buy rating on the shares and bumped his price target from $240 to $345, suggesting the stock will deliver returns of 21.5% in the months ahead. (To watch Ellis’s track record, click here)

Ellis sees the appointment of Durn as an excellent choice. Before joining Adobe, he served as CFO at multiple semi companies, including GlobalFoundries, Freescale Semiconductor, NXP Semiconductors, and Applied Materials. “Our interaction during his Applied Materials tenure left us favorably impressed with financial execution during a period of geopolitically challenged growth. We believe his capital allocation and strategic and operational-oriented experience fit MRVL’s AI build-out well,” the 5-star analyst said.  

But that’s not the only reason Ellis is bullish here. Following the company’s May 27 FQ1 results, CEO Murphy gave his first COMPUTEX keynote, during which Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang made an unscripted appearance and proclaimed that Marvell will be “the next $1 trillion company.”

Marvell and Nvidia’s partnership includes collaboration on custom silicon through Nvidia IP enablement, wider adoption of NVSwitch and NVLink Fusion, joint development efforts involving Celestial and in-house silicon photonics, as well as 6G and AI-RAN initiatives. After the earnings release, Ellis believed FY28’s $16.5 billion revenue target reflected some contribution from NVLink Fusion, although he also thought the revenue opportunity could be substantially larger, a view he continues to hold.

Lastly, on Friday, June 5, it was announced that Marvell would be added to the S&P 500 ahead of trading on June 22, replacing Pool Corp. The stock climbed roughly 10% the following Monday, a move that Ellis thinks was likely driven by passive index-related buying ahead of the inclusion. A place in the market’s benchmark index not only “broadens the NT and LT ownership base,” but also caps a “remarkable rise” from a $5 billion market cap when Murphy joined around mid-2016 to ~$250 billion, representing a “significant accomplishment” in his tenure.

Turning now to the broader Street view, where most of Ellis’s colleagues are also MRVL supporters. With an additional 23 Buys and 4 Holds, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Strong Buy. However, the $252 average price target implies the stock will decline by 11% over the next year. That disconnect likely reflects more on how sharply the stock has rallied than on any weakening in conviction, suggesting that more upward target revisions are likely not be far behind. (See MRVL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: The GPU Opportunity Is Here: Citi Upgrades AMD Stock
data.description:
With shares up by 312% over the past year, Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) has been one of the market’s strongest performers.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

However, not everyone has been convinced in the story. For example, Citi’s Atif Malik, an analyst ranked in 2nd spot among the thousands of Street stock experts, has kept a skeptical stance on the chipmaker until now.

But “until now” is the correct wording here given the 5-star analyst has now changed his tune. Based on “increased conviction in AMD’s GPU business and server CPU tailwinds,” Malik has upgraded his AMD rating from Neutral to Buy, while his price target goes from $460 to $575, implying the stock will gain 18% over the coming months. (To watch Malik’s track record, click here)

While Malik has been “constructive on the inflection in agentic AI led CPU demand” with AMD positioned as the primary beneficiary, he now views the company as a credible second source in the GPU market, with the company positioned to capture the “lion’s share” at Meta.

The analyst expects Meta to become a “significantly larger customer” of AMD’s AI products, particularly GPUs, than consensus expectations currently imply.

The analyst believes that deployment of custom MI450 GPUs is likely to result in lower TCO (total cost of ownership) for Meta vs. merchant GPU offerings. Recall, AMD announced a six-gigawatt, four-year Meta agreement involving 160 million common stock warrants for custom MI450 GPUs, with the first 1GW tranche set to ramp in 2H26 and 2027. The analyst reckons that each gigawatt corresponds to $15 billion of revenue for AMD. As such, Malik has raised his AI sales estimates and now forecasts $33 billion in 2027 (up 137% year-over-year) and $50.8 billion in 2028 (up 54% YoY).

Additionally, following Computex, Malik has raised his CPU 2030 TAM (total addressable market) model from $132 billion to $137 billion and still expects AMD to remain the “key beneficiary of the CPU renaissance,” driven by advantages in performance leadership, expanded core counts, the x86 instruction set architecture, a wider SKU range, and capabilities spanning both multithreaded and single-threaded workloads.

When comparing AMD’s upcoming Venice CPU to rival offerings from Nvidia (Vera), and Intel (Diamond Rapids), AMD’s product comes out on top.

The analyst expects AMD’s Venice to span roughly 8 to 256 cores, allowing for a “variety of workloads” while sustaining performance leadership and supporting diverse compute demands.

Intel is also expected to see success, but Malik sees its Diamond Rapids CPU as trailing Venice due to the absence of multithreading, lower core counts, and an anticipated overall “performance gap.” Intel’s follow-up Coral Rapids CPU is expected to reintroduce multithreading, though it is likely not due until 2028.

Nvidia should be competitive in reasoning server CPUs with Vera, which incorporates a proprietary spatial multithreading approach, but is limited to 88 cores compared with Venice scaling up to 256 cores and Diamond Rapids up to 192 cores, and is built on the ARM ISA. ARM-based systems can face legacy software compatibility constraints and can require application porting, although the analyst notes this issue is gradually diminishing.

Given Nvidia’s fabless model and fixed wafer allocations from foundry partners, Malik still expects the company to prioritize GPU shipments over standalone CPU volumes due to its “entrenched position” in the GPU market.

So, that’s the Citi view, but what does the rest of the Street have in mind for AMD? Based on an additional 27 Buys and 7 Holds, the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. However, the $89 average price target implies the stock is fully valued. That likely reflects more the speed of the stock’s recent jump than any deterioration in conviction, implying that more upward revisions to price targets could follow shortly. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: ‘Don’t Ignore These Risks,’ Says Top Analyst About Super Micro (SMCI) Stock
data.description:
As the saying goes, a rising tide lifts all boats, but in the case of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI), it might not be high enough.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

At least that is the opinion of Wolfe Research analyst George Notter, an analyst who ranks among the top 3% on Wall Street and has initiated coverage of the AI server maker with a Peer Perform (i.e., Neutral) rating without offering a fixed price target. (To watch Notter’s track record, click here)

While Notter concedes that AI “rising tides” help the company, he also says there are “lots of risks here.”

On the plus side, Notter thinks the “asymmetry in the AI infrastructure market is abundantly clear.” Those with supply will benefit in a market where everyone needs AI infrastructure.

But while he remains strongly supportive of the broader market opportunity, he also sees “significant risks” for Super Micro. Expansion among major customers, namely CoreWeave and xAI, is expected to increase customer concentration risk, while the inherently unpredictable nature of AI data center deployments reduces the degree of business visibility.

Moreover, profitability could face headwinds, with customer concentration expected to remain a factor affecting the gross margin mix. Historically, the company has shown a readiness to capture market share even when doing so weighed on profitability. Yet Notter thinks the two margin drivers highlighted by Super Micro, DCBBS (Data Center Building Block Solutions) and Enterprise mix, are not likely to contribute as much to the financial model as investors might be hoping for.

That said, HPE’s withdrawal from lower-margin Neocloud contracts, together with Dell’s continued ability to maintain 5-6% operating margins in AI, could establish a floor for the market. Super Micro also appears to be placing greater emphasis on margin optimization, an area that had not previously received the same level of attention.

But elsewhere, Notter also points to governance issues, regulatory exposure, and an uneven track record in setting and subsequently meeting expectations as additional areas of concern. Not to mention, the DOJ recently indicted co-founder Wally Liaw over allegations related to illegal AI server shipments into China, a situation that could potentially destabilize the company. “We think this is a low probability, albeit worth considering for investors,” the 5-star analyst went on to say.

Potential risks here could include the departure of the company’s auditor, BDO USA, and/or additional turnover within the senior leadership team. Notably, no other executives have been implicated in the indictment at this stage, and Super Micro has stated that its relationship with Nvidia has not been affected. Demand likewise does not appear to have suffered, with backlog levels reported to be at “record highs.”

Nevertheless, Notter says that these factors “limit the multiple we’re willing to place on the business despite the improved growth.”

So, that’s the Wolfe view, but what does the rest of the Street think lies in store for SMCI? Based on a total of 10 Holds, 3 Buys and 2 Sells, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Hold. That said, the $35.55 average price target offers 12-month upside of 22%. (See SMCI stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: From Bear to Bull: Intel (INTC) Stock Gets a Double Upgrade From Bank of America – Here’s Why
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) stock has been enjoying a shift in sentiment that has made it one of the AI trade’s biggest winners. Even after a recent pullback, the stock has gained 418% over the past 12 months.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Now it seems one prominent market watcher has also become an Intel bull. That is BofA’s Vivek Arya, an analyst who ranks among the top 1% on Wall Street, and whose change of heart is particularly notable. Arya has just double-upgraded his INTC rating from Underperform (i.e., Sell) to Buy, while also increasing his price objective from $96 to $135, suggesting the stock will gain 26% in the months ahead. (To watch Arya’s track record, click here)

The 5-star analyst’s new bull thesis is based on “higher confidence in INTC’s opportunity to help address industry constraints in leading edge wafers/packaging, plus supply into a much larger agentic CPU TAM.”

Overall, Arya now estimates total IDM CY30 EPS power at $6+ compared with a prior $3–$4 range, although the analyst stresses that continued execution across both product and foundry segments remains critical.

Arya also highlights the recent CDNS ‘14A node’ IP agreement alongside Terafab engagement activity as “additional supportive data points,” which contribute to improving “longer-term visibility” within external foundry and support a more durable IP ecosystem.

Within Products, Arya now forecasts INTC server CPU revenue reaching ~$40 billion+ by CY30E, representing roughly a ~25% share of a $170 billion TAM (total addressable market). For Foundry, Arya points to “potential INTC engagements,” including Apple M-Series wafers, MediaTek TPU wafers, Terafab IP and packaging work, other ARM-based server CPUs, and broader edge AI expansion into the Client segment.

Meanwhile, Arya also notes that Intel ranks as the second least owned (after Sandisk) semiconductors/AI infrastructure stock in the S&P 500 as of May 2026, despite its ~$540 billion market cap placing it fifth within the peer group. At 16% ownership, he sees broader ownership participation as potentially resulting in share price appreciation, similar to AMD, where ownership increased +1,400 basis points year-over-year to 39% in May (with AMD stock rising +309% YoY).

Arya does flag some risks, which include increasing ARM and custom CPU competition, possible moderation in AI capital expenditure affecting CPU demand, and execution risk in leading-edge design and foundry operations. Additional constraints include power and cost pressures. “Still, with CPU TAM up ~40% and its roles structurally expanding, we expect a high-conviction, multi-year upgrade cycle ahead,” the analyst summed up.

Arya now joins 10 other analysts in the bull camp, yet with an additional 25 Holds and 2 Sells, the stock claims a Hold consensus rating. The average price target clocks in at $91.44, implying the stock is now overvalued by 14.5%. (See Intel stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Rivian (RIVN) Launches the R2 – Is It the Big Catalyst? Cowen Weighs In
data.description:
Rivian’s (NASDAQ: RIVN) R2 has finally arrived. On Tuesday, the EV maker said it had begun the first public customer deliveries of its new R2 mid-size SUV. The lower-priced SUV is designed to attract buyers away from Tesla and other competitors. The first R2 vehicles produced at the company’s Normal, Illinois plant are the performance variant, with pricing starting at $57,990. Rivian stated that the R2 Premium model will launch in late 2026 at around $54,000, followed by the R2 Standard version in 2027, priced at $44,990.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

So, what are the vehicle’s chances of success in today’s market? Having attended the R2 launch event in Irvine, California, Cowen analyst Itay Michaeli came away with “greater conviction” in his R2 demand thesis.

“We continue to believe R2 checks all the right boxes for future success: (1) Reasonable price range in a popular segment. (2) Compelling features & premium content. (3) A no compromise EV SUV. (4) Robust AV/ADAS roadmap. (5) A well-timed launch with our US EV thesis,” the analyst said.

The design studio tour highlighted several ways Rivian reduced R2 BoM (bill-of-materials) costs without “resorting to de-contenting.” By applying learnings from the R1 and making use of Rivian’s “deep vertical integration” and software capabilities, the R2 achieved an “impressive efficiency profile,” including vs. major EV competitors, resulting in a well-equipped SUV without compromises. Efficiency gains from R2 are expected to be carried over into the next-generation R1.

Management also reiterated confidence in demand for the R2 while still viewing supply as the primary risk to the ramp, though no specific supply constraints were highlighted. Early demand is already broad-based across different trade-in types and price segments, which Michaeli sees as an “encouraging early sign.”

Meanwhile, the launch timing for the $45,000 Standard rear-wheel drive variant was brought forward to summer 2027 from late 2027, a change driven by improved visibility into the production ramp rather than a shift in demand expectations. Management actually indicated they expect relatively low take-up for this trim, but that’s not an issue for the analyst. “We view the earlier entry of the ~$45k trim as a positive in that it could draw more consumer traffic even if buyers ultimately opt for upper trims,” Michaeli said on the matter.

On autonomy, Rivian’s roadmap remains on track, with point-to-point (level 2+) functionality targeted for this year, “eyes-off” capability expected in 2027, and level 4 autonomy – covering robotaxi and consumer applications – planned for 2028.

“We continue to view a successful H2 ramp as a catalyst for the stock,” Michaeli summed up.

All told, Michaeli maintained a Buy rating on the shares along with a $20 price target, implying the stock will gain 29% in the months ahead. (To watch Michaeli’s track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, RIVN stock claims an additional 7 Buys, 5 Holds and 2 Sells, for a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The average price target clocks in at $18.53, implying shares will post one-year returns of 20%. (See RIVN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 6 Signs Tesla (TSLA) Has Cracked the Self-Driving Code, According to Piper Sandler
data.description:
Skepticism around Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) FSD (full self-driving) software remains widespread.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

Piper Sandler analyst Alexander Potter notes that in discussions with clients, Waymo’s larger robotaxi deployment is cited as evidence Tesla may not be ready. Furthermore, the debate is complicated by the absence of “universally-accepted,” apples-to-apples data on disengagements, crashes, and other key performance indicators.

Even so, Potter argues that Tesla has “effectively achieved Level 4 autonomy (at least in most conditions)” and points to 6 developments that support his view.

For one, Tesla has begun actively promoting its insurance offering, including providing lower premiums to customers who frequently use FSD. As a long-time Tesla owner in Minnesota, where Tesla Insurance has been available since early 2022, Potter questions why the company would incentivize FSD usage through insurance discounts if it believed the technology was unreliable.

He also highlights Tesla’s investment in the Cybercab program. Volume production started in April, with plans to manufacture hundreds of vehicles per week, and multiple Cybercabs have already been spotted. Potter argues that building dedicated production lines likely requires hundreds of millions of dollars in capital, making little sense if Tesla lacked confidence in FSD.

Another factor is Tesla’s effort to secure permits for robotaxi infrastructure. Potter points to a proposed facility in Irving, Texas, near Dallas, that would be used to store and charge robotaxis. The site includes more than 200 parking spaces and 16 Supercharger stalls. “Facilities like this must necessarily predate fleet expansion,” Potter opined.

He further notes that Tesla has started disclosing FSD subscription figures. Historically, the company’s investor relations team avoided sharing FSD adoption rates, describing them as “noise, not signal.” That changed in 1Q26, which Potter interprets as a sign that FSD adoption has become a meaningful indicator of Tesla’s business performance and that the technology is “ready for dissemination beyond early adopters.”

Potter also points to Tesla’s plans to expand its robotaxi service into additional cities during the first half of 2026. While he acknowledges the timeline “might be unrealistic,” he thinks the “trend is clear.” Tesla’s robotaxi service is already available across the Austin metropolitan area, including interstate routes.

Finally, Potter cites his own experience with the technology. In April, his Tesla completed a drive from Missoula to Minneapolis using FSD. Having owned Teslas exclusively since 2016, he believes the latest versions are capable of genuine self-driving in many situations, particularly under favorable weather conditions. He adds that a recently shared cross-Canada FSD road trip provides another example of the technology’s capabilities.

All that is to say, Potter maintained an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating on TSLA shares, backed by a $500 price target. The implication for investors? Upside of 27% from current levels. (To watch Potter’s track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 11 Buys, 14 Holds and 3 Sells, for a Moderate Buy consensus rating. At $404.54, the average target offers modest upside of 3.5%. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: ‘Load Up’: Applied Digital (APLD) Stock Remains One of AI’s Best Bets, Says Top Analyst
data.description:
Applied Digital’s (NASDAQ:APLD) deal-making activity is grabbing the headlines again after the AI infrastructure firm announced on Monday that it had nabbed a third billion-dollar hyperscaler agreement.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Two ETFs to trade APLD long or short: APLX & APLZ

The company said it signed a new 15-year take-or-pay lease for its newly announced Delta Forge 2 campus in a southern U.S. state with the same investment-grade hyperscaler, on broadly similar terms and pricing to its two most recent agreements. The contract covers 210 MW of critical IT load and is anticipated to deliver ~$5.2 billion in contracted revenue over the base term, rising to ~$12.7 billion if all renewal options are exercised across a potential 30-year relationship.

“Notably,” said Northland’s Mike Grondahl, an analyst who ranks among the top 4% on Wall Street, “this represents the third lease signed with the same investment-grade hyperscaler and makes ~70% of contracted revenue now backed by U.S. based investment-grade hyperscalers.”

APLD has now leased three sites totaling 810 MW in under 50 days, bringing total contracted capacity to 1.41 GW of critical IT load and roughly ~$36 billion in base-term lease revenue, increasing to ~$86 billion if all renewal options are exercised. The company previously indicated in January that it was in advanced discussions with a top-tier investment-grade hyperscaler for up to three sites, which have now all been fully leased. Additionally, following the PF3 lease announced on May 20, APLD said it was marketing ~1.7 GW of additional grid-connected capacity across both newly sourced and existing campuses.

Grondahl believes there’s plenty more good news on the way. The AI infrastructure buildout cycle is still early, with several potential “valuation catalysts” ahead. First, execution risk is expected to decline in 2026–2027 as projects are delivered, with current lease announcements pushing valuation toward ~15x average net operating income, while fully delivered and stabilized assets could justify closer to ~20–25x EBITDA once “execution risk is removed.”

Second, stabilization within 6–12 months of live data center operations could enable refinancing at materially lower borrowing costs across billions of dollars of debt. Third, a potential REIT conversion could improve tax efficiency. Finally, ongoing site sourcing and expansion opportunities at existing contracted campuses are expected to support further leasing activity, which Grondahl thinks the Street is “significantly undervaluing.”

“We see APLD as one of the first companies within our coverage universe to transition through these catalysts and experience positive re-ratings from the market for their leased capacity,” the analyst summed up. “Overall, we believe APLD’s management, site sourcing, and development team is executing at an extremely high level right now, and we have the utmost confidence in APLD to continue rapidly growing its platform.”

APLD is “the real deal,” adds Grondahl, maintaining an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares alongside a $56 price target. That suggests the stock will gain 33% over the next 12 months. (To watch Grondahl’s track record, click here)

The Street’s average price target is actually quite higher – at $69.11, the figure makes room for one-year returns of 64%. On the rating front, based on a unanimous 10 Buys, the analyst consensus naturally rates the stock a Strong Buy. (See APLD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Was Apple Stock’s (AAPL) WWDC a Success? Analysts Offer Differing Verdicts
data.description:
Following many delays and repeated setbacks, Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) finally unveiled a significant upgrade to Siri at its WWDC event on Monday. The voice assistant will be integrated with Apple’s AI offering, Apple Intelligence, and has been renamed “Siri AI.”

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The updated Siri is designed to function more like AI chatbots such as ChatGPT or Google Gemini rather than a conventional query-and-response assistant that primarily pulls information from the web. It is set for a broad release in the fall.

Apple also said it has redesigned how its devices manage children’s accounts, with a stronger focus on safety and more parental control over usage. The new protections restrict the content children can access, who they can communicate with, and when they can use certain apps.

Alongside the upgraded Siri, Apple highlighted several additional Apple Intelligence features. These include AI integration across Safari, Messages, and the Home app. The company also unveiled new tools for the Camera and Photos apps that use AI to generate and modify images, with these capabilities developed in partnership with Google.

Taking it all in, Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring said that while Apple did not “check all the boxes,” WWDC 2026 still represented a “clear step in the right direction.”

The analyst believes that WWDC 2026 showcased a noticeably upgraded and rebuilt Apple Intelligence platform alongside improved Siri functionality, greater clarity around the timing of feature rollouts, and “clearer paths to monetizing AI” through both hardware and Services. However, he also pointed to shortcomings, including limited third-party app integration, no availability in the EU or China (which accounts for 35% of trailing twelve-month iPhone shipments), and only limited advanced agentic workflows.

Nevertheless, Woodring, who ranks among the top 2% of Street stock experts, has raised his fiscal year 2027 EPS estimate slightly from $10.23 to $10.30, reflecting “slightly clearer and nearer-term monetization opportunities.” He also applies a valuation multiple more in line with Apple’s historical peak at 35 times earnings, citing what he describes as a “clear AI strategy” that he expects to strengthen over time.

Overall, Woodring says WWDC 2026 highlighted tangible progress on Apple’s AI roadmap and suggested earlier monetization opportunities than Woodring had previously expected, while also reinforcing that improvements to Apple Intelligence will be a “marathon, not a sprint.”

“With less backwards compatibility + clearer use cases, we see WWDC as a net positive,” the 5-star analyst summed up.

To this end, Woodring maintained an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares, while his price target goes from $330 to $360, implying the stock will gain 19% over the coming year. (To watch Woodring’s track record, click here)

On the other hand, KeyBanc analyst Brandon Nispel viewed the event as disappointing, arguing that the AI updates fell short, especially against what he considered to be a “high bar for advancements of Apple Intelligence.”

“We saw: no clear signs of monetization of AI; Apple’s reliance on Google Gemini; very few use cases where Apple App integration was materially beneficial; and a slightly better standalone Siri, which is still worse than other LLMs,” Nispel opined. “WWDC was where Apple Intelligence was supposed to drive an upgrade cycle, but this seems to no longer be the case, even though some of the most advanced on-device model features require iPhone 17 or newer.”

All told, Nispel reiterated a Sector Perform (i.e., Neutral) rating, without offering a fixed price target. (To watch Nispel’s track record, click here)

9 other analysts join Nispel on the sidelines, but an additional 18 Buys and 1 Sell all add up to a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Going by the $322.6 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 7% premium. (See Apple stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Can Apple (AAPL) Stock’s AI Revolution Kick Off at WWDC? Daniel Ives Weighs In
data.description:
It’s a big day in the Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) universe. The company’s WWDC (Worldwide Developers Conference) gets underway at Apple Park in Cupertino, California. While the annual extravaganza is aimed at developers, it traditionally offers a glimpse into upcoming software releases and, at times, new hardware initiatives.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

This year’s conference also carries added significance as it will be the final Apple event led by outgoing CEO Tim Cook. Cook is set to deliver Monday’s keynote for the last time before he hands leadership duties to longtime hardware boss John Ternus.

Unlike prior developer conferences, however, this year’s WWDC is not expected to be assessed primarily on new devices or software updates. Instead, all attention will turn to Apple’s progress in AI, an area where many believe the company has lagged competitors.

As such, Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives believes WWDC represents a “pivotal moment” in Apple’s future. “In this AI World Apple has more cash, consumers, and brand recognition than any company in the world…but now is the time to flex the muscles and go on the offensive instead of the defensive,” the 5-star analyst said.

A key theme of the event will be Apple’s AI-powered Siri chatbot. Expected developments include a dedicated Siri application and new capabilities arriving through iOS 27, allowing Siri to incorporate personal context through a redesigned interface. Apple also revealed last week that Gemini had been chosen to power Siri’s AI functionality beginning in September, with models operating both on Apple devices and within its private cloud compute infrastructure. Google’s technology was selected because it was viewed as the strongest base for Apple Foundation models. “We believe this is the first step for Apple to create a foundational AI consumer platform that enables Apple users to start integrating Google’s Gemini AI model into Apple’s hardware ecosystem,” Ives, who ranks among the top 4% of Street stock experts, opined on the matter.

Ives also touched upon the CEO handover, noting that the event serves as an important milestone for Ternus ahead of his expected appointment as CEO later this year. In Ives’ view, Ternus is unlikely to take a “treadmill approach,” which he sees as a positive development. “We strongly believe this will be the start of AI monetization period of the Apple ecosystem as the company will continue to lay the foundation through its new operating system updates across mac, iOS, iPad, and others with its iOS 27 update,” Ives summed up.

All told, Ives maintained an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares, backed by a Street-high $400 price target. The implication for investors? Upside of 30% from current levels. (To watch Ives’ track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 17 Buys, 10 Holds and 1 Sell, for a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Going by the $320.83 average price target, shares will appreciate by a modest 4% over the next year. (See Apple stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: ‘Brace Yourself,’ Says Top Investor About Micron (MU) Stock
data.description:
Micron (NASDAQ:MU) stock might have endured a brutal sell-off in Friday’s tech rout, but it should be put into perspective. Even after the 13% pullback, shares of the computer memory maker are still up 203% year-to-date and by 679% over the past year.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The thing is, that drawdown might actually present an opportunity to load up before the stock takes off once again.

That is the takeaway from recent comments by top investor Harsh Chauhan, who ranks among the top 1% of investors on TipRanks and writes for The Motley Fool.

Chauhan predicts that the stock could “go parabolic” after June 24. That’s when Micron will deliver its fiscal 2026 third-quarter and all signs point to an outstanding print.

Chauhan’s bullish take is based on the premise memory prices are “poised to keep growing at an incredible pace.”

AI-driven demand for DRAM and NAND memory has far outpaced supply, as data centers require huge amounts of high-speed memory for AI training and inference workloads. Memory manufacturers have increasingly shifted production toward HBM used in AI accelerators, tightening the supply of conventional memory chips for PCs and smartphones because HBM consumes roughly three times more wafer capacity than standard DRAM.

At the same time, surging AI-related storage needs have pushed HDD lead times out to late 2027 and absorbed much of the available enterprise SSD supply, further straining the NAND market. As a result, memory prices continue to climb sharply. TrendForce expects DRAM prices to rise another 58%-63% in 2Q26 after a 98% jump in Q1, while NAND flash revenue surged 3.5x vs. the year-ago period, with Gartner projecting NAND prices could jump 234% in 2026. “All this suggests Micron could crush Wall Street’s expectations once again when it reports results,” the 5-star investor said.

That will continue a recent trend, as Micron has “handsomely crushed” Wall Street’s earnings forecasts over the past four quarters. For fiscal Q3, the company is calling for revenue of $33.5 billion at the midpoint of its guide, representing a huge 3.6x increase from the same period last year. It also expects adj. EPS to reach $19.15, up 10x YoY.

With memory prices surging throughout the first half of the year and supply constraints expected to remain in place “until the end of the decade,” Chauhan believes Micron looks well positioned to outperform its current outlook when it reports results on June 24. Despite those growth drivers, the stock trades at only 6 times forward earnings, suggesting the market “may not have fully priced Micron’s growth potential into the stock price.”

“That’s why growth-oriented investors can consider buying Micron’s shares leading into its quarterly report, as its stunning rally is likely to receive a major shot in the arm after June 24,” Chauhan summed up. (To watch Chauhan’s track record, click here)

That bullish view gets support from the Street’s analysts. Micron claims a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on a mix of 26 Buys vs. 3 Holds. However, the $860.2 average price target implies the shares are fully valued. That likely reflects the stock’s strong climb rather than any weakening in analyst confidence, suggesting that higher price targets could follow soon. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: New Chip Star Cerebras Systems (CBRS) Stock Gets a Street-High Price Target From Citi – Here’s Why
data.description:
Cerebras Systems (NASDAQ:CBRS) made a splashy market entrance last month, with shares jumping 68% on May 14, its first day of trading on Nasdaq. The stock saw out the session at $311.07, significantly above its IPO price of $185 per share. The offering raised $5.55 billion, ranking it among the largest U.S. tech IPOs in recent times.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Two ETFs to trade CBRS long or short: CBRX & CBRZ

The stock has since retreated by 35%, but Citi’s Atif Malik, an analyst ranked in 3rd spot among the thousands of Street stock experts, sees plenty of gains ahead for the newly listed chipmaker. Malik initiated coverage of CBRS with a Buy rating and a Street-high $340 price target, implying the stock will deliver returns of 64% in the months ahead. (To watch Malik’s track record, click here)

Cerebras designs processors for AI workloads, with an emphasis on inference tasks where models generate outputs in response to user inputs. Its flagship product, the Wafer Scale Engine 3, departs from conventional chip design by using an entire silicon wafer as a single integrated processor rather than linking multiple chips together, as is typical in Nvidia’s GPU-based architecture. Cerebras claims this design improves both speed and efficiency when handling AI inference operations.

The TAM (total addressable market) covering the full AI inference infrastructure stack is substantial and expanding quickly. Malik estimates the AI inference chip market will increase from $113 billion in 2025 to $649 billion by 2030, implying a 42% CAGR (compound annual growth rate), driven by improvements in large language models and agentic AI as companies push toward real-time generative AI deployment and hyperscalers scale up infrastructure for compute-intensive, data-driven workloads. Within this broader market, Malik forecasts the fast inference segment will reach $130 billion by 2030.

And within this segment, Malik believes Cerebras has the “first mover advantage.”

The company operates specifically in the latency-sensitive part of the inference stack, where speed is critical, and this “fast-inference” segment is growing faster than the broader market as reasoning models see wider adoption.

Malik expects that inference workloads will account for over 60% of total AI compute spending by the end of this year, highlighting a broader shift in demand away from training toward inference as real-world AI applications scale.

Cerebras is poised to make the most of this opportunity. “Within the fast inference market TAM of $130B by 2030, we expect Cerebras leveraging its unique wafer scale technology and mostly serving OpenAI, AWS, and others to be the market leader and achieve 40-50% of the market with likely Nvidia through its upcoming LPX products (via Groq acquisition) and Google via its recently announced TPUi platform right behind Cerebras,” the 5-star analyst said.

As for any potential risks, Malik counts competition, high customer concentration with OpenAI accounting for more than 70% of sales through 2028, and execution risk related to scaling 2 GW of cloud service capacity as the key ones to monitor.

So, that’s the Citi view, but what does the rest of the Street have in mind for Cerebras? All analysts are on board. Based on a unanimous 9 Buys, the stock naturally claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $290.83 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 40% premium. (See CBRS stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Is a $1 Trillion Valuation for Marvell (MRVL) Stock Outlandish? Not According to This Investor
data.description:
After a brief hiatus, Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL) stock is once again surging, up by 13% in Monday’s trading. The move follows Friday’s announcement that the chipmaker will be added to the S&P 500 later this month, with inclusion scheduled for June 22.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Monday’s jump is an impressive one, but not quite as big as the 32% climb the stock took last week after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Marvell is poised to become the next $1 trillion company.

Investor Dmytro Lebid notes that while the forecast may be bold, factoring in the “anticipated pace of AI infrastructure expansion, this is a realistic projection.”

The key question around Huang’s announcement is what the Nvidia CEO’s underlying motive is for promoting a company that competes directly for hyperscaler spending. With that in mind, Lebid has identified three core reasons Marvell’s growth is “beneficial for NVDA’s future valuation.”

For one, from a partnership and ecosystem angle, Nvidia’s $2 billion investment and deeper collaboration with Marvell through NVLink Fusion reflect an effort to tightly integrate networking and compute ecosystems. The focus is on scaling AI infrastructure for hyperscalers, where high-speed interconnects are becoming a key bottleneck. “Marvell has been doing great in this area, making its tech a perfect fit for scaling Nvidia’s ecosystem,” says Lebid.

Second, Marvell is emerging as a key player in optical and photonics-based connectivity, which Nvidia views as ever more central to next-generation data center architecture. Marvell’s early momentum in this space, including acquisitions such as Polariton Technologies and the launch of a 102.4 Tbps switch with improved energy efficiency, positions it as a strong partner in advancing ultra-fast interconnects required for AI workloads.

Third, Lebid thinks that a higher valuation for Marvell could help Nvidia “better withstand antitrust scrutiny.” A stronger, more valuable Marvell reinforces the perception of a competitive and diversified AI hardware ecosystem, which could help ease antitrust scrutiny on Nvidia by reducing concerns about dominance. It also reduces the risk of Marvell becoming a weakened target for rivals like AMD or Intel. “Nvidia benefits from having a strong, independently operating tech giant nearby that can modestly share the AI industry’s large pie with NVDA,” Lebid further said.

All told, Lebid thinks that the recent “explosive growth” of Marvell, alongside Huang’s public comments, appears to go beyond “speculative hype” and instead signals structural shifts in AI infrastructure design. Marvell is well positioned to form new strategic partnerships that sit at the intersection of major compute infrastructure providers and the “new global economic order.”

At the same time, Lebid concedes that current valuation multiples suggest Marvell is trading at a significant premium relative to Nvidia and Broadcom. “However,” the investor summed up, “I believe the company’s market value will continue to rise justifiably based on fundamental catalysts such as Wall Street’s upward revisions to its forecasts and Marvell’s inclusion in the S&P 500 index.”

Accordingly, Lebid rates MRVL stock a Buy. (To watch Lebid’s track record, click here)

On the Street, this stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on a mix of 24 Buys vs. 4 Holds. However, the $247.96 average price target implies the stock is overvalued by 17%. That disconnect likely reflects more on the speed of the stock’s recent run-up than any weakening in conviction, implying that upward revisions to price targets may soon follow. (See MRVL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: SNDK, STX and WDC: Mizuho Raises Price Targets for AI Memory Stocks – Here’s Why
data.description:
Memory stocks’ brief slump appears to be over for now, with leading sector names pushing higher in Monday’s trading.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

STXX: an alternative to margin or options on STX

The uptick comes against a backdrop of bullish AI-driven sentiment on Wall Street. One analyst showing confidence in the memory segment’s ongoing strength is Mizuho’s Vijay Rakesh, who ranks in 4th spot among the thousands of Wall Street stock experts. “We continue to see AI as the driving force behind the supply-demand imbalance in the memory market, as we note increasing demand in 2027/28E could add further pressure to the market,” the 5-star analyst said.

Rakesh remains constructive on both the DRAM and NAND markets. For DRAM, he forecasts wafer starts to increase 10% year-over-year in 2026 and 6% in 2027, mostly driven by rising HBM demand. However, he notes that the growing share of HBM production is likely to moderate overall supply growth. Demand is projected to expand by 27% in 2026 and 24% in 2027.

Within NAND, the analyst identifies enterprise SSDs (eSSD) as the “key driver of demand.” He estimates total NAND demand will grow 18% YoY in both 2026 and 2027, while wafer starts are expected to decline 5% in 2026 before rising 3% in 2027. Supply should remain constrained, with no meaningful new capacity expected to come online until 2028.

At the same time, Rakesh has bumped his price targets higher for several names in the space.

For Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK), the analyst reiterated an Outperform rating and raised his price target from $1,825 to $2,220, based on ~11x his fiscal 2027 EPS estimate vs. roughly 10x previously. The new figure suggests 12-month share appreciation of 30%. (To watch Rakesh’s track record, click here)

Rakesh believes the stock remains attractively valued relative to the 2027 SOX multiple of about 23x earnings, supported by “pricing tailwinds” that could extend through 2027, helping sustain gross margins above 80% and driving EPS growth of roughly 3x year-over-year.

“SNDK and other players are benefiting from demand outpacing supply in the NAND market, driving higher pricing, as AI continues to drive tailwinds across handset, PC and server (eSSD) end markets with Reasoning/Answer Tokens driving 2M+ Context windows and LLM/MMM data generation, while Agentic AI continues to ramp,” the 5-star analyst explained.

Sandisk gets a Strong Buy consensus rating on the Street, based on 14 Buys vs. 2 Holds. At $1,843.44, the average target points toward 12-month returns of 9.5%. (See SNDK stock forecast)

data.title: ‘This Is Compelling’: Citi Pounds the Table on Eli Lilly (LLY) Stock
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) shares are getting the thumbs up from investors on Monday after new data on its next-generation obesity therapy retatrutide strengthened confidence in the pharma giant’s ability to maintain its leading position in the expanding weight-loss market.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

At the American Diabetes Association meeting in New Orleans, the company disclosed full results from two studies of retatrutide, including one in treatment-naive type 2 diabetes patients and another in individuals with obesity. In a Phase 3 trial, the highest 12 mg dose of retatrutide produced weight loss of 28.3% after 80 weeks. Even at a lower 4mg dose, the drug delivered roughly 19% weight reduction, placing it in a similar range to big seller Zepbound.

Following the event, Citi analyst Geoff Meacham noted that although multiple datasets supported clinical relevance, retatrutide “stood out the most.” The analyst says that the 4 mg dose producing about 19% weight loss is a result “worth mulling over.” He added that this level exceeds semaglutide’s 2.4 mg dose and approaches the upper end of tirzepatide’s approved 15 mg dosing, while maintaining placebo-like dropout rates. “What used to be an asset relegated by most on the Street to be a niche product serving patients BMI 35+, it’s become clear the 4mg dose offers compelling efficacy, tolerability, and ease of (i.e., relative lack) titration that could see its broader adoption among a much larger patient population and prescribing set,” the analyst went on to say.

Meacham also adds that retatrutide’s “near-term catalyst density is the sharpest in the class,” with four additional Phase 3 readouts expected in 2026 alone. A regulatory submission for obesity is targeted for the second half of 2026, followed by a type 2 diabetes filing in the first half of 2027.

Further milestones include a head-to-head study vs. tirzepatide in 2027 and additional chronic low back pain data next year too. Expansion into metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD), cardiac outcomes, and kidney-related endpoints is also anticipated through 2029–2030, reinforcing the case that retatrutide’s “commercial utility is only just getting started to be understood.”

Overall, the analyst came away with “conviction on Lilly firmly intact.” The analyst notes that both retatrutide and Foundayo, an oral GLP-1–based obesity treatment, have programmes with “enough indication breadth to make them category-defining franchises across cardiometabolic and obesity-led complications.”

Additionally, Eloralintide, an investigational amylin receptor agonist designed to reduce appetite and increase satiety, adds a “genuinely differentiated layer.” Early Phase 2 results suggest it may offer strong efficacy alongside favorable tolerability, which in the analyst’s view has not yet been “appropriately credited” by the market. “With market leading share and a pipeline architecture that is one of the most compelling in large-cap Biopharma, we see continued momentum for LLY shares,” Meacham summed up.

To this end, Meacham maintained a Buy rating on the shares, while his Street-high $1,500 price target offers 12-month upside of 28%. (To watch Meacham’s track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 19 Buys, 2 Holds and 1 Sell, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $1,267.57 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 8.5% premium. (See LLY stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: ‘This Is Underappreciated’: Tesla (TSLA) Stock Gets an Upgrade From J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is positioned to be a leader in physical AI, and is heading into new and largely unproven TAMs (total addressable markets). Execution, though, will be critical not only for adoption, but also for expanding the size of those markets themselves.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

So says J.P. Morgan analyst Rajat Gupta, who has now upgraded the firm’s TSLA rating from Underweight (i.e., Sell) to Neutral. The price target also gets a bump, rising from $145 to $475, suggesting the stock will gain 13.5% in the months ahead. (To watch Gupta’s track record, click here)

Gupta believes Tesla’s big advantage is deep vertical integration across hardware and software at an industrial scale, which remains difficult to match. This structure supports faster development cycles and better coordination across its technology stack. “We believe this aspect, while largely known at a high-level, is still somewhat under-appreciated and misunderstood, for the sheer starting-point advantage it brings,” the analyst said.

Gupta says that using cell production and vehicle manufacturing facilities as a proving ground for Optimus and other humanoids should reduce cost of goods sold in the core automotive business, while more importantly “validate the product at industrial scale” and lower unit costs for broader enterprise and commercial deployment of humanoids. He’s talking about a large market opportunity, with Gupta estimating roughly 5 million humanoids in the United States and around 30 million globally by 2040E.

The same line of thought is applied to the robotaxi. With extensive existing driving data of about 10 billion miles and a personal vehicle fleet of roughly 9 million currently on the road, expanding autonomous vehicle operations across additional cities and regions should improve coverage of edge cases and boost camera-only vision performance. Gupta expects this will “help generate significant network-effects” across both robotaxi usage and pricing, while also supporting broader FSD (Full Self-Driving) adoption across an estimated 35 million personal TSLA fleet and around 40 million robotaxis by 2040E.

At the same time, the resulting gains from R&D efficiency and manufacturing productivity, including a potential roughly 5% reduction in automotive cost of goods sold from Optimus-related benefits, could enable “significant pricing competitiveness” in the core vehicle business and create optionality for renewed EV demand and higher supercharger utilization, with additional feedback loops into FSD and robotaxi adoption.

Moreover, Energy storage also benefits from structural demand drivers such as data center expansion and grid stability requirements over the coming years, while Gupta thinks broader adoption across Tesla’s ecosystem will further accelerate residential solar and storage adoption, creating “another feedback loop into the business.”

Over time, the underlying infrastructure could also generate “significant value” through software licensing for FSD and Optimus, distributed inference using compute from the personal vehicle fleet, robotaxi fleet, and Optimus units, Cortex compute resources, and supercharger network utilization.

So, that’s the J.P. Morgan view, but what does the rest of the Street have in mind for TSLA? Based on an additional 12 Buys and Holds, each, plus 4 Sells, the stock claims a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The $404.54 average price target suggests the shares will see modest downside of 3% over the next year. (See Tesla stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why CrowdStrike (CRWD) Stock Is Tanking Today and Why It Just Got a New Street-High Price Target
data.description:
CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) is being subjected to a beating in Thursday’s market action with the stock down by 7% as of writing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The pullback is in response to the cybersecurity specialist’s fiscal first-quarter results, which were actually very strong.

Revenue came in at $1.39 billion, up 26.4% year-over-year and ahead of expectations by $30 million. At the other end of the scale, adj. EPS reached $1.10, beating the estimates by $0.03.

For the second quarter, the company guided for adj. EPS of $1.16 to $1.17, slightly above the $1.16 the Street was after. Revenue guidance of $1.43 billion to $1.44 billion also matched expectations of $1.43 billion.

So, what’s the downbeat sentiment all about? Rosenblatt analyst Catharine Trebnick has an answer. “We believe this sell-off is likely the result of profit taking on the strong news after shares rose ~87% since the last earnings report,” the analyst said.

For Trebnick, however, that matters little, as the company delivered an “outstanding” report, marked by a clear “beat and raise” across all guided financial metrics.  

“Highlighting a monumental industry inflection point termed the ‘Mythos moment,’ the intersection of frontier AI models and cybersecurity has positioned the Falcon platform as critical AI infrastructure,” the analyst further said.

Among the standout metrics, Trebnick highlights the record Q1 NNARR of $256 million (+32% YoY) and total ending ARR rising to $5.51 billion (+24% YoY). The total revenue gain also represented a fourth consecutive quarter of sequential top-line acceleration.

Profitability also reached new highs, with record non-GAAP operating income of $326 million (24% margin) and record free cash flow of $468.5 million, equal to 34% of revenue.

Additionally, driven by sustained structural demand for AI-layer security, management raised full-year net new ARR growth guidance by more than 500 basis points and also announced its first public 4-for-1 forward stock split.

As a result, Trebnick reiterated a Buy rating on the shares and raised her price target from $640 to a new Street-high of $825, suggesting the stock will gain 19% in the months ahead. The new target is based on a 29x EV/2027 sales multiple, amounting to a premium over security peers, which currently trade at approximately 12.2x.

“We believe CrowdStrike is deserving of this multiple,” Trebnick summed up. “The Falcon platform is a strategic accelerator, and enterprises can’t safely deploy their multi-trillion-dollar AI roadmaps without CrowdStrike’s underlying security layer.” (To watch Trebnick’s track record, click here)

Most of Trebnick’s colleagues are bulls too; the stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on a mix of 31 Buys, 6 Holds and 1 Sell. However, the $705.64 average price target implies the stock will stay rangebound for the time being. With this in mind, watch out for further model tweaking from analysts over the coming days. (See CRWD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Rocket Lab (RKLB) Stock Gets a New Street-High Price Target – Here’s Why
data.description:
Rocket Lab’s (NASDAQ:RKLB) big rally is on hold for now. Shares of the space company had been on a tear but have been showing signs of exhaustion. To wit, over the past week, the stock has fallen by 24% from its peak.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Yet, according to Clear Street analyst Greg Pendy, investors need not worry given the bull case remains as compelling as ever. In fact, the analyst has not only reiterated a Buy rating on the shares, but has also raised his price target from $98 to a new Street-high of $129, suggesting the stock will gain 12.5% over the coming months. (To watch Pendy’s track record, click here)

Pendy’s positive investment thesis rests on Rocket Lab’s transition from a launch-focused provider into a “vertically integrated space platform” that combines launch services, satellite manufacturing, and mission-critical components, creating a “differentiated competitive moat.” The analyst forecasts approximately 38% revenue CAGR (compound annual growth rate) through 2030E, driven by three main factors.

First, Space Systems growth, which accounted for the majority of 2025 revenue, is being supported by deeper vertical integration and a move toward “higher-value” prime contractor roles, contributing to margin expansion. Second, Electron continues to dominate the small-lift launch category, with higher launch frequency and solid unit economics driving both top-line and margin growth. Third, Neutron represents a “major catalyst” by expanding Rocket Lab’s addressable market and positioning it as a “credible alternative” to SpaceX for national security and constellation missions, with launch revenue anticipated to meaningfully accelerate from 2027 onwards.

Pendy also points to a “structural launch deficit” that is creating a “multi-year capacity vacuum” in the space launch market.

Novaspace projects over 43,000 satellites will be launched by 2035, averaging 12 satellites and 8 tons of payload per day, supporting a $665 billion market in satellite manufacturing and launch services.

However, launch capacity is not keeping pace with demand. This is already visible in Amazon’s request for a two-year FCC extension for its LEO constellation, claiming that it is producing satellites faster than they can be launched due to grounded rockets, long development cycles, and constrained spaceport capacity. Amazon operates a factory able to produce around 30 satellites per week, but has struggled to secure sufficient launch availability. “This market constraint is the crux of the Neutron opportunity: if it flies successfully in late 2026, it enters a market where SpaceX is maxed out through 2027 and Blue Origin is currently grounded for potentially 12+ months,” the analyst explained.

Furthermore, Rocket Lab’s three-pad network across two continents is a strategically important and underappreciated asset, offering orbital flexibility, range independence, and geopolitical diversification. Even with increased rocket supply, the U.S. launch sector remains constrained by bottlenecks at heavily oversubscribed federal ranges like Cape Canaveral and Vandenberg, compounded by regulatory frictions such as FAA licensing, environmental reviews, and range safety constraints. In contrast, Rocket Lab’s New Zealand site operates under a separate regulatory regime, offering “unobstructed downrange trajectories in almost any direction,” while its Wallops facility provides a critical domestic U.S. capability for sensitive government missions.

So, that’s the Clear Street view, but how do the rest of the Street’s analysts see the next year panning out for RKLB? Based on an additional 9 Buys and 4 Holds, the stock claims a Moderate Buy consensus rating. However, the $101.5 average price target now sits 11.5% below the current share price. With this in mind, watch out for further model tweaking from analysts shortly. (See RKLB stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: IREN Stock Gains an Edge Over the AI Infrastructure Pack – Canaccord Explains Why
data.description:
IREN (NASDAQ:IREN) has been a recent market favorite, with shares of the bitcoin miner-turned data center operator up by 649% over the past year. The stock is grabbing headlines again today on the back of two positive developments.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

IREN has a long & short ETF? Explore IREX & IREZ

IREN announced plans for an 800MW data center campus in South Australia, with the site expected to kick off operations in 2028. The AI infrastructure company said the campus will be supported by submarine fiber connections linking it to major Asia-Pacific markets, including Singapore, Indonesia, South Korea, and Japan.

Meanwhile, in a new research note, Canaccord analyst Joseph Vafi raised his price target from $70 to $79, implying the stock will gain another 14% over the next year. Vafi, who ranks among the top 3% of Street stock experts, maintained a Buy rating on the shares. (To watch Vafi ‘s track record, click here)

“IREN continuing to trailblaze even against an industry backdrop that is moving pretty fast in its own right,” the 5-star analyst effusively said.

Vafi’s praise comes in the wake of Monday’s announcement that IREN had secured a $3.65 billion investment-grade financing facility, which will fund the remaining capital expenditures needed to support its $9.7 billion multi-year AI Cloud Services agreement with Microsoft.

Notably, says the analyst, although financing conditions for AI-related infrastructure investments have become increasingly favorable, IREN is the first company in the space to achieve investment-grade status, earning ratings of A from Fitch and A(low) from DBRS. While Vafi points out that those ratings were undoubtedly supported by its Microsoft contract, the significance for future fundraising efforts should not be sniffed at.

Investment-grade status gives IREN access to a much larger pool of capital than is typically available through high-yield debt or convertible equity financing. “We believe the rating agencies analyzed a number of factors here, including not only the creditworthiness of MSFT as an offtake partner, but also IREN’s track record to date in delivering AI cloud services” Vafi sent on to say. “We also believe the forecasted cash flows from this project also support this rating.”

With financing for the Microsoft project now secured, Vafi revised his return analysis upward, reflecting stronger-than-expected economics. IREN secured up to $3.65 billion in funding at SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate) + about 2.2%, which was below his prior cost of capital assumptions and improved the project’s return profile. SOFR is a US benchmark interest rate that reflects the cost of short-term borrowing secured by US Treasury securities.

As such, he estimates the project could generate an IRR (internal rate of return) of about 32% over the life of the Microsoft contract (5 years). If the GPUs remain useful for an additional two years beyond the contract and retain roughly half their original value, the seven-year IRR increases to about 36%. While the difference appears small in percentage points, IRR is a compounding measure, so even modest increases can translate into meaningfully higher total returns over time.

So, that’s the Canaccord view, but how does the rest of the Street see the next year panning out for IREN? Based on an additional 6 Buys, 3 Holds and 1 Sell, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Moderate Buy. Going by the $75.70 average price target, the shares will appreciate by 9% in the year ahead. (See IREN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: KeyBanc Weighs In on Microsoft (MSFT) Build: 5 Key Takeaways
data.description:
Microsoft Build, Microsoft’s (NASDAQ:MSFT) annual developer-focused conference, is currently underway, with CEO Satya Nadella opening the event with a keynote that set a strongly AI-focused tone, highlighting Microsoft’s push to embed AI more deeply into everyday computing workflows.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader was taking notes and points to five key takeaways from the event.

First, agent integration into daily workflows remains a priority. Microsoft introduced Project Solara, described as a chip-to-cloud platform designed to support agentic experiences across devices. The company also previewed two new device concepts – one desktop-based and another wearable badge – both “increasing potential agentic interactions at your desk and on the go as part of daily workflows.” The goal is to enable an always-on agent layer that follows users across form factors rather than being confined to a phone or PC. “As agent use continues to expand, we view this announcement as another means of Microsoft agents to become further embedded in users’ workflows, enhancing productivity within the Microsoft platform,” Ader said on the matter.

Second, Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman unveiled seven new AI models spanning transcription, voice, and image editing. He highlighted improvements in performance, efficiency, and cost relative to competing systems such as Gemini and OpenAI. A standout for Ader was MAI-Thinking-1, Microsoft’s first reasoning model built for low token cost efficiency. These models are being run on Microsoft’s Maia 200 chips, which the company says deliver a 1.4x performance-per-watt improvement versus GB200 hardware. The Maia chips will also support M365 Copilot. “As silicon co-design helps to cut costs, we view this positively as Microsoft competes against the likes of Amazon’s Trainium and Google’s TPU chips,” Ader further said.

Third, Microsoft “reinforced the innovation” on both cloud and edge infrastructure. Nadella emphasized that modern workloads increasingly span training, inference, and agent runtime across distributed systems. At the edge, new PC-level compute capabilities involving CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs are central to what Microsoft describes as a “local agentic loop.”

Microsoft also introduced a unified IQ data layer, integrating Fabric IQ, Work IQ, Foundry IQ, and the newly announced Web IQ. This layer is designed to improve context awareness for agents by incorporating structured and unstructured data, including web content, video, and imagery. The objective is to improve response quality while reducing latency and token consumption. “As agents grow, we continue to view having a contextualized semantic data layer as a key component for agents to run most efficiently at the lowest cost,” Ader explained.

Finally, the tech giant unveiled Majorana 2, a topological quantum chip designed to improve qubit reliability by a factor of 1,000. Microsoft now targets a scalable quantum computer by 2029, reducing their prior goal in half. Alongside this, Microsoft Discovery was introduced as an R&D platform aimed at helping researchers apply AI models to complex scientific problems with greater contextual support. “As AI capabilities and agentic AI increase rapidly, we see Microsoft continuing to prioritize the quantum computing project in the longer term as there are more resources to tackle quantum and research problems still at hand,” Ader summed up.

All told, there’s no change to Ader’s Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating or $600 price target, a figure suggesting shares will gain 36% in the year ahead. (To watch Ader’s track record, click here)

It’s a similar story among Ader’s colleagues; based on a mix of 35 Buys vs. 2 Holds, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Strong Buy. The average target stands at $557.64, implying the stock will generate returns of 26% a year from now. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock Is Down Today, and Why HSBC Remains Bullish
data.description:
Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares are under pressure in Tuesday’s trading after the company announced plans to raise as much as $80 billion, a move that is likely to reignite debate around the long-term economics of the AI buildout.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The capital raise is intended to help fund AI infrastructure, computing capacity, and employee-related tax obligations. The financing package includes $30 billion of underwritten offerings, consisting of $15 billion in mandatory convertible preferred stock and $15 billion in common shares. Additionally, Alphabet plans to launch a $40 billion at-the-market (ATM) program for its Class A and Class C shares, with sales expected to begin in the third quarter. Separately, the company has agreed to sell $10 billion of stock to Berkshire Hathaway in a private placement.

Alphabet expects to use the ~$40 billion raised through its public offerings and the private placement primarily to expand its AI infrastructure and computing capacity, while also covering general corporate expenses. Meanwhile, the ATM program will help support a change in how the company handles taxes on employee stock awards. Going forward, Alphabet will pay these tax obligations with corporate cash rather than through share withholding, with the total expected to reach up to $30 billion in 2026.

HSBC analyst Paul Rossington points out that Alphabet has already reduced share buybacks and tapped the bond markets to fund higher capex and potential M&A in 2026, while also signaling a further “significant increase” in capex in 2027. Against that backdrop, for Rossington, the announcement of a new capital raise this early came as a surprise. He adds that it contributes to already “elevated fundraising/IPO activity” across the broader AI sector, which now includes Anthropic – having confidentially filed an intention to float on June 1- and comes ahead of an expected filing from OpenAI. “So as not to be put at a competitive disadvantage, we cannot rule out further capital raises from other hyperscalers looking to build out AI infrastructure / compute capabilities against what appears to be a backdrop of strong investor demand,” Rossington summed up.

Nevertheless, Rossington thinks the company remains “uniquely placed to benefit from AI,” and he maintained a Buy rating on the shares. That said, his price target goes from $435 to $420, now suggesting the stock will gain 15% in the months ahead. (To watch Rossington’s track record, click here)

The Street’s average target is only a little higher – at $427.89, the figure suggests the shares have one-year upside potential of 17%. On the rating front, based on a mix of 28 Buys vs. 5 Holds, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Strong Buy. (See GOOGL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
